title: Honey-Glazed Roots & Halloumi Salad
summary: A no-faff salad built from a spiced roasted veg and crispy halloumi traybake, finished with pomegranate, chickpeas, rocket and creamy hummus.

method.description: Peel the carrots and parsnips, then cut into long, even-sized chunks. Remove the tough core from the parsnips.
Grease a large roasting tin with oil. Tip in the carrots and parsnips, then toss with most of the olive oil, cumin seeds, a generous pinch of salt and black pepper. Roast for 10 minutes.

Meanwhile, cut the halloumi into chunks and drain the chickpeas. After 10 minutes pop the halloumi on top of the veg and drizzle with the honey. Roast until everything is tender and starting to turn golden, for another 10 mins.
Drain the chickpeas, and toss with the rocket, pomegranate seeds and roasted veg and halloumi. Serve with dollops of hummus and dig in.

chefs.name: Anna Helm Baxter
chefs.summary: Anna Helm Baxter is a cookbook author, food stylist, and food producer at Mob. Having spent most of her life in the US, she loves potlucks, tailgates, and a good Thanksgiving spread. As a working mum of three, she gets the dinner struggle, which is why she’s all about recipes that are joyful, healthy, and totally doable. And if all else fails she always has cookies stashed in the freezer.
